data.body:
## Context

Developers use RED markers in .test-index to track tests that are expected to fail during TDD — the marker tolerates failures at or after a boundary test function so RED tests don't block commits. When the GREEN implementation lands and those tests start passing, the marker should be removed. In practice, markers are often forgotten, accumulating stale entries that obscure which tests are genuinely in RED phase. We just manually cleaned up 8 stale markers across 6 test files. The test gate should catch this automatically by failing when RED-zone tests pass for changed files — the same trigger mechanism used for failing GREEN tests.

## Success Criteria

- When record-test-status.sh runs a test file that has a RED marker and the file exits 0, the status is recorded as failed with a clear 'stale RED marker' message identifying the test file and marker name
- When record-test-status.sh runs a test file that has a RED marker, the file exits non-zero, and any test at or after the RED marker line passes, the status is recorded as failed with a message identifying the specific passing RED-zone test(s)
- When record-test-status.sh runs a test file that has a RED marker, the file exits non-zero, and all RED-zone tests fail (current expected behavior), the status is recorded as passed (existing tolerance preserved)
- The detection adds no new scripts, hooks, or validation phases — it extends the existing RED zone logic in record-test-status.sh

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Extend the existing RED zone logic in record-test-status.sh with two checks: (1) exit 0 + RED marker = instant fail (all RED tests passed, marker is stale), (2) exit non-zero + RED marker = after the existing failure-position check, also parse passing tests and verify none are at or after the RED marker line. Requires a parse_passing_tests_from_output function mirroring the existing parse_failing_tests_from_output.
